meta.title: U11 Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW X1 U11 engine — B38, B48, B47, PHEV, iX1 electric. Real UK costs, honest reliability data, and a clear 70% BMW threshold. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW X1 U11 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned U11 engine typically costs £1,800–£8,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], depending on engine — the B38 three-cylinder sits at £1,800–£3,500, the B48 four-cylinder at £2,800–£6,500, and the PHEV at £4,000–£8,000. The iX1 electric motor replacement costs £8,000–£12,000. Fitting adds £650–£1,170. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW X1 U11 engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol in xDrive23i form (218 bhp) is the most reliable U11 engine — it's proven across BMW's UKL platform, avoids the B38's injector issues and the B47 diesel's DPF sensitivity, and offers strong performance. The iX1 electric powertrain is likely to be reliable but is too new for long-term data.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW X1 U11?

acceptedAnswer.text: Common issues include: injector failure on the B38 three-cylinder petrol; cooling system failures (water pump, thermostat) on the B48 petrol; DPF clogging and timing chain wear on the B47 diesel; battery degradation and cooling system leaks on the PHEV variants; and general electronic sensor issues across all variants.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW X1 U11 reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Overall, yes — the U11's B48 petrol engines are modern and highly reliable. The B38 has well-documented injector issues but they're manageable with proactive maintenance. The B47 diesel is significantly more robust than its N47 predecessor. The PHEV and iX1 are more complex but well-supported by BMW's network.

mainEntity.name: Which U11 engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: We don't recommend outright avoidance — but a B38 without documented injector history is one to approach with caution. Injector failure is common and can lead to piston damage if ignored. For diesels, avoid any B47 used primarily for short urban trips where DPF clogging is more likely. For the PHEV, ensure the battery health is documented.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my U11 engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key warning signs: B38 — rough running, misfires, or check engine light (injectors); B48 — coolant loss with no external leak, overheating, or water pump noise; B47 — DPF warning light, loss of power, or timing chain rattle; PHEV — reduced electric range, battery warning lights; iX1 — battery warning lights, charging faults. Use our diagnostic guide to identify the specific symptoms.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW X1 U11 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply our 70% BMW threshold. A £4,800 B48 recon against a £30,000 U11 is just 16% — well within the threshold. Even the M35i's £6,500 recon against a £38,000 car is just 17%. Unlike older BMWs, the U11's high retained value means engine replacement is almost always financially viable. Value the car first — but on a modern U11, a recon is usually a repair decision, not a financial one.

mainEntity.name: How long do BMW X1 U11 engines last?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 petrol can exceed 150,000 miles with proper servicing. The B38 is similarly durable when injector issues are addressed proactively. The B47 diesel is robust and can reach 150,000+ miles with regular maintenance. The PHEV's battery health is the limiting factor — expect 8–10 years of service before significant degradation. The iX1's battery is covered by an 8-year/100,000-mile warranty.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between the U11 and U12?

acceptedAnswer.text: The U11 is the standard-wheelbase BMW X1, produced from 2022 to the present. The U12 is the long-wheelbase version, produced for certain markets (China and potentially others). Both share the same engine lineup and platform, but the U12 offers increased rear legroom. The U11 is the version sold in the UK and Europe.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW X1 U11 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation; block stamping varies by engine. For the B38/B48, the code is on the block near the exhaust manifold. For the B47, it's on the block face below the inlet manifold. Tell us your registration and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted BMW specialists will confirm it for free.

hero.h1: BMW X1 U11 Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every engine fitted to the third-generation X1 — from the 1.5-litre B38 three-cylinder petrol to the 2.0-litre B48 and B47 four-cylinders, plus the plug-in hybrid xDrive25e and xDrive30e, and the all-electric iX1. Real UK replacement costs, honest reliability data, and the cooling system and injector issues that define this generation's ownership story. Backed by real UK enquiry data [PATTERN DATA].

confidenceScore.title: Confidence Score
confidenceScore.text: All ratings are provisional — no dedicated Engine Page exists for any U11 engine family. The B48 four-cylinder petrols are considered the most reliable based on their proven track record across BMW's UKL platform. The B38 three-cylinder has known injector issues. The B47 diesel is significantly more robust than its N47 predecessor. The iX1 electric powertrain is too new for long-term reliability data.

overview.intro: The U11 is the third generation of the BMW X1, a subcompact luxury crossover SUV launched in October 2022. Built on the BMW UKL2 platform, it is available with a wide range of powertrains: B38 three-cylinder and B48 four-cylinder petrols (including mild-hybrid variants), B47 four-cylinder diesels, B38-based plug-in hybrids (xDrive25e and xDrive30e), and the all-electric iX1. A long-wheelbase version (U12) is also produced for certain markets. The U11 is offered with front-wheel drive (sDrive) or all-wheel drive (xDrive) across most engine variants. A Life Cycle Impulse (LCI) update is planned for 2027.
overview.keyFacts: Platform: BMW UKL2 (front-wheel-drive-based) • Predecessor: F48 • Successor: TBA • Sibling body styles in this generation: U12 (long-wheelbase).
overview.marketIntelligenceLine: The U11 X1 remains a popular choice in the UK premium compact SUV segment, with strong demand for the sDrive18i petrol and xDrive23d diesel variants [PATTERN DATA].

items.quote: Thin-Data Fallback mode — exactly one paragraph: The B48 2.0-litre four-cylinder petrol in xDrive23i form (218 bhp) is the sweet spot of the U11 range — strong performance, proven reliability across BMW's UKL platform, and without the B38 three-cylinder's injector concerns or the B47 diesel's DPF sensitivity. The M35i is thrilling (300–312 bhp) but carries higher running costs and more strain on the engine. The B47 diesels are solid, torque-rich options for high-mileage buyers, but they're increasingly rare in the UK market. The iX1 electric is the future-proof choice, but battery replacement costs remain high (£8,000–£12,000). For most buyers, the xDrive23i petrol is the sensible, reliable choice.

rows.ownershipVerdict: Budget Choice — cheap to run but watch the injectors.
Mild Hybrid Choice — economical but complex.
Best Petrol — proven, reliable, and the sweet spot.
Performance Choice — thrilling but expensive to maintain.
Diesel Mild Hybrid — refined but discontinued in many markets.
Future-Proof Choice — low running costs, high replacement cost.

economicsRule.title: The U11 rule
economicsRule.text: A £4,800 B48 recon against a £30,000 U11 sits at 16% — well within our 70% BMW threshold. Even the M35i's £6,500 recon against a £38,000 car is just 17%. Unlike older BMWs, the U11's high retained value means engine replacement is almost always financially viable. Value the car first — but on a modern U11, a recon is usually a repair decision, not a financial one.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U11 Costs Least To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: sDrive18i (B38) — cheap to run, but budget for injector maintenance.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U11 Costs Most To Own?
keyTakeaways.answer: iX1 — battery replacement is expensive; M35i has high fuel and maintenance costs.

keyTakeaways.question: Which U11 Is Best Value?
keyTakeaways.answer: xDrive23i — the sweet spot of performance, reliability, and running costs.

problems.title: B38 Three-Cylinder Injector Failure
problems.rootCause: The B38's direct fuel injectors can fail due to heat-soak issues — when the engine is switched off, fuel in the injectors can evaporate, causing them to overheat and become stuck or leak. Symptoms include rough running, misfires, and check engine lights.
problems.recommendation: At the first sign of rough running or a misfire on a B38 U11, have the injectors inspected immediately — a £400–£1,000 injector replacement is far cheaper than the £2,500–£4,000 engine replacement that follows if a failed injector causes piston damage.

cta.label: Compare B38 engine prices for your U11 →

problems.title: B48 Cooling System Failure
problems.rootCause: The B48 four-cylinder petrol engine suffers from water pump failure and cooling system leaks. The plastic coolant pipes and thermostat housing can become brittle with age and heat cycling, leading to coolant loss and potential overheating. The M35i's high-output B48 places additional strain on the cooling system.

tieredCosts.work: Water pump replacement; coolant system inspection

problems.recommendation: Have the cooling system inspected at every service — a £400–£900 water pump replacement is far cheaper than the £2,800–£4,800 engine replacement that follows an overheating failure.

cta.label: Compare B48 engine prices for your U11 →

problems.title: B47 Diesel DPF and Timing Chain Issues
problems.rootCause: The B47 diesel is significantly more robust than its N47 predecessor, but DPF clogging from short urban trips is a common issue. Timing chain wear can also occur on higher-mileage cars with neglected servicing.
problems.recommendation: On any B47 diesel U11 approaching 40,000 miles, have the DPF inspected and ensure regular motorway regeneration. A £600–£1,500 preventative service is far cheaper than the £2,800–£4,800 replacement that follows a major failure.

cta.label: Compare B47 engine prices for your U11 →

problems.title: PHEV Battery Degradation
problems.rootCause: The 16.3 kWh lithium-ion battery pack can degrade over time, reducing electric range and overall performance. The cooling system for the battery and electronics can also develop leaks.

tieredCosts.work: Battery health check; cooling system service

problems.recommendation: On any PHEV U11 approaching 30,000 miles, have the battery health checked and the cooling system inspected — a £600–£1,500 preventative service is far cheaper than the £6,000–£9,000 battery replacement that follows a major failure.

cta.label: Compare PHEV battery and engine prices for your U11 →
